rules.description: AI/ML development standards - PyTorch, TensorFlow, GPU optimization, model management, reproducibility
AWS CloudWatch best practices - logging, metrics, alarms, dashboards, and CloudTrail standards
AWS Fargate (ECS) best practices - task definitions, service configuration, container security, and logging standards
AWS Well-Architected Framework best practices - general security, reliability, performance, cost optimization, and operational excellence standards
AWS OpenSearch best practices - domain configuration, index management, query optimization, access control, and audit logging standards
AWS RDS PostgreSQL best practices - database configuration, connection management, authentication, backup, and performance optimization standards
Critical anti-patterns quick reference - condensed rules for fast context loading
Documentation and markdown creation policy - when to create .md files, Diátaxis structure, and strategic docs for agents
Core engineering philosophy and values
Universal language-agnostic rules to prevent common LLM-induced anti-patterns across all programming languages and frameworks
Git commits, branches, rebase/merge, and PR titles/descriptions for clean, maintainable contributions
Go-specific development standards following Effective Go, Go Code Review Comments, and Uber Go Style Guide
Build automation rules for SweetPad/xcodebuild integration and automated verification workflows
UI/UX and SwiftUI best practices - HIG compliance, presentation APIs, layout patterns
Java-specific development standards following Effective Java, Google Java Style Guide, and modern Java 17 LTS best practices
Java-specific development standards following Effective Java, Google Java Style Guide, and modern Java 21 LTS best practices
JavaScript/ECMAScript development standards following modern best practices, Airbnb/Google style guides, and JavaScript community conventions
Obsidian CLI commands and plugin dev loop when user interacts with a vault
Obsidian vault anti-patterns for OFM notes, Bases, and JSON Canvas
OWASP-aligned secure coding: input validation, output encoding, auth, session, access control, crypto, logging, data protection, DB, file, memory
Python-specific development standards following PEP 8, PEP 20 (Zen of Python), and Pythonic best practices
Rust development standards: idioms, style, anti-patterns (Rust 2021 edition, 2024 where applicable)

Operate pi-spine batches in Cursor: spine CLI commands, diagnosis, recovery, gates, integrate, and report upstream engine bugs. Use when user asks to start/pause/resume spine batch, run spine doctor/preflight/plan, check batch status, approve gate, recover from orphans, or report spine bugs—not when authoring packets or implementing task code.

Create pi-spine task packets (PROMPT.md, STATUS.md, dependencies.json) under the configured tasks root. Use when asked to create spine tasks, decompose PRDs for spine batch, or stage SP-* work—not when operating batches or implementing code.

Manually execute a pi-spine task packet in Cursor when no spine batch is active: honor Contract, Testing step, File Scope, STATUS discipline. Use when user @PROMPT.md under spine tasks root and asks to implement—not when spine batch is running or user wants packet authoring.
Stet CLI usage - commands and dismiss reasons for Cursor and stet-assisted review
Swift 5.9 development standards - SwiftUI, layers, concurrency, testing; platform minimum from project deployment target
Swift 6 development standards - strict concurrency, SwiftUI, layers; platform minimum from project deployment target
Create Taskplane-style task packets (PROMPT.md, STATUS.md, CONTEXT.md) for autonomous execution outside Cursor. Use when asked to create, stage, queue, or plan tasks—not when implementing code.

Execute a Taskplane-style task packet in Cursor: read PROMPT.md and STATUS.md, stay within File Scope, update checkboxes honestly, run verification before claiming done. Use when implementing or executing a task—not when creating new task packets.
